text.title: aws-cli-setup Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for aws-cli-setup. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Install AWS CLI v2 (not v1) on Linux by downloading the official zip from awscli.amazonaws.com and running the bundled installer. Verify with `aws --version` showing `aws-cli/2.x`.
rule.rationale: AWS CLI v2 supports SSO, IMDSv2, and all current API features. v1 is no longer recommended and lacks SSO integration.

rule.statement: Use named profiles (one per account/environment) rather than overwriting the default profile. Always specify --profile or set AWS_PROFILE when running commands against non-default accounts.
rule.rationale: Named profiles prevent accidental operations in the wrong account. A default profile pointing to production is a common source of destructive incidents.
